name: setec-voiceprint
description: Text-only stylometry for authorial voice in prose — not audio voice identification. Stylometric tools for AI-prose smoothing diagnosis and authorial-voice coherence comparison. Six task surfaces: smoothing diagnosis, authorial-voice coherence, validation + calibration, craft restoration, discrimination evidence (uncalibrated by default), and narrative-decision audit (Russell et al. 2026 / StoryScope literature anchor, uncalibrated by default). Python tooling spans variance audits, length-matched bootstrap percentiles, sliding-window localization, authorial-voice profiling, idiolect preservation lists, authorial-voice drift tracking, per-POV authorial-voiceprints, manifest validation, corpus-hygiene checks, per-bigram KL decomposition, named-pattern density audit, R stylo oracle, voice-validation harness, calibration toolchain (bake-off matrix runner + length-stratified subsampling + slicer/polarity-audit chainer + per-comparator + per-(judge × generator) direction routing), metric-targeted restoration packets, before/after restoration verdicts with metric-gaming detection, MVP empirical validation harness, impostor-corpus acquisition from live blogs / Blogger Takeout / online magazines / PDF libraries with optional OCR, author-facing voice insights report generation, Binoculars two-model perplexity audit (Hans et al. 2024), the external-mirror multi-LLM continuation-distance evidence pack, and the StoryScope 30-core-feature narrative-decision audit with a pluggable LLM judge and a cross-corpus polarity-check workflow. The 'voice' here is authorial voice in prose (the writer-level patterns essayists and developmental editors talk about), borrowed from authorship-attribution stylometry; it is not vocal spectography or speaker recognition.
